status: internal diligence review, not buyer-facing copy
input_note: No actual artifacts were pasted to Claude, only descriptions of their existence and the pricing/positioning. This review is based on the stated package structure and pricing posture, not a line-by-line read of the actual documents.
overall_verdict: The pricing is not yet earned by the evidence described. A high-price ask for a framework whose own status line says not externally validated, not a benchmark, and not a certification will face serious buyer skepticism. This is not mainly a messaging problem; it is an evidence gap.
deal_killers: No third-party validation, pilot results, or reference customer exists yet.
The $3M-$5M+ license tier implies a maturity curve not yet supported by evidence.
The suite is locally schema-validated, which is structural validation, not market, outcome, or technical-performance validation.
No comparable market anchor yet exists for pre-deployment architectural logic diagnostics.
major_buyer_objections: Show me a completed engagement and outcome.
What happens if your review misses a failure and the workflow later fails in production?
How is this different from internal red-teaming?
Why should we trust a 25-case synthetic suite to represent our actual production risk surface?
Who delivers this if the founder is unavailable?
pricing_objections: The price reads as arbitrary without a comparable anchor.
The jump from diagnostic pilot to $3M-$5M+ license is too large before referenceable pilots.
At $250k-$500k, buyers will compare against established consulting, assurance, and technical review firms.
technical_diligence_risks: Synthetic-only scenarios may not transfer cleanly to real workflows.
Failure taxonomy and rubric are internally generated and not yet externally cross-checked.
Version-control and schema-iteration issues must not be exposed as buyer-facing immaturity.
legal_or_ip_risks: IP protection posture must be clearer before licensing conversations.
False-negative liability allocation must be handled contractually.
Non-certification and non-benchmark status must appear in contracts and proposals, not only internal notes.
procurement_risks: Large-enterprise procurement may require vendor-level certifications or security questionnaires.
High-value engagements need milestones, acceptance criteria, and termination/off-ramp terms.

claims_to_remove_or_soften.current_claim_or_phrase: Pre-Deployment Architectural Logic Stress-Testing and Constraint Mapping Framework
claims_to_remove_or_soften.risk: May imply stronger rigor or coverage than currently evidenced.
claims_to_remove_or_soften.recommended_replacement: Early-stage diagnostic framework for surfacing architectural failure patterns in agentic workflows

claims_to_remove_or_soften.risk: Price and licensing posture are ahead of evidence before any referenceable pilot.
claims_to_remove_or_soften.recommended_replacement: Remove from first outreach and reserve for later qualified conversations after pilot evidence exists.

claims_to_remove_or_soften.risk: Internal completeness is not the same as buyer-ready market validation.
claims_to_remove_or_soften.recommended_replacement: structured diagnostic methodology seeking a first paid pilot engagement

fix_now_before_outreach: Drop the $3M-$5M+ tier from current outreach materials.
Lead with one narrow, bounded paid diagnostic pilot only.
Add explicit non-certification and non-benchmark disclosure to any proposal or engagement document.
Prepare a one-paragraph business-continuity answer for solo-founder risk.
Keep internal versioning and schema-iteration issues out of buyer-facing collateral.
later_validation_needed: Complete at least one full diagnostic pilot with a referenceable outcome.
Establish inter-rater reliability or external cross-check on rubric dimensions.
Cross-check the taxonomy against external AI failure-mode or incident literature.
Develop vendor procurement readiness if targeting large enterprises.
Build a comparable-price anchor or cost-of-alternative argument.
minimum_credible_paid_pilot_package: Narrow scope against a named workflow and a named subset of failure categories.
Explicit written non-certification and non-benchmark disclosure.
Clear deliverable-acceptance milestones and off-ramp condition.
Simple continuity plan if the founder becomes unavailable.
best_defense_of_value: The defensible claim is not that this is a validated test. The defensible claim is that this is a disciplined, structurally rigorous first-pass diagnostic methodology for surfacing failure patterns that many internal review processes do not explicitly check.
failure_column: This diligence verdict stops holding if a completed referenceable pilot, signed LOI, market validation, or early-access buyer profile already exists and was not included in the source context.
final_recommendation: Do not lead outreach with the full tiered pricing structure or upper-tier license language. Lead with a single, narrowly scoped diagnostic pilot, explicit non-certification/non-benchmark disclosure, and a clear deliverable-acceptance structure.
